title: DTN buffer uses time.time() instead of monotonic clock
description:
**Location:** python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:434-436, 475-478

**Bug:** The DTN buffer expiration uses `time.time()` (wall clock) for expiry comparison. If the system clock jumps (NTP sync, manual change, DST), messages may expire immediately or live forever.

**Trigger:** System clock jumps backward (messages never expire) or forward (messages expire prematurely).

**Bad behavior:**
- Clock jump backward: DTN buffer fills up with 'unexpired' messages that should have been removed
- Clock jump forward: Valid messages are discarded before their intended lifetime

**Pattern:** The spec likely intends expiry to be monotonic. Use `time.monotonic()` and convert Unix timestamps to monotonic offsets at message insertion.
